# Pull request

## Summary

<!-- 1-3 sentences: what does this change do? why? -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Refactor (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Security fix
- [ ] Build / CI / tooling

## Test plan

<!-- How did you verify this works? Include commands run, scenarios tested. -->

- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] Tests added or updated (if touching auth, points/items, admin actions, migrations, or other critical paths)
- [ ] CI passes

## Documentation

<!-- The single most important hygiene checkbox. Tick exactly one. -->

- [ ] Docs updated — `README` / `/docs/features/` / `/docs/architecture/` / new ADR / new runbook
- [ ] N/A — no doc impact

## Repo-root hygiene

- [ ] No new `.md` files added to the repo root (only `README`, `CONTRIBUTING`, `SECURITY`, `CHANGELOG`, `LICENSE`, `CLAUDE.md` are allowed at root)

## Changelog

- [ ] Added an entry under `## [Unreleased]` in `CHANGELOG.md`
- [ ] N/A — internal-only change

## Related

<!-- Closes #X, refs #Y, follows up on PR #Z, etc. -->

<sub>By submitting this PR you confirm you've read [`/CONTRIBUTING.md`](/CONTRIBUTING.md).</sub>
